spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the feature's general idea but doesn't surface the named spec API symbols directly.

interactive.rationale: No real interactive surface — this reads as a styled code card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Page covers the promises the feature index makes for this concept.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is covered but only a few failure modes are surfaced.

references.rationale: Page does not cite the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link — required self-heal anchor.

design_system.rationale: Has 4 raw hex colour value(s) inline — should use --bg-*/--text-*/--accent-* tokens instead.

openQuestions.title: Surface more named spec API for update-device-memory-api-limits
openQuestions.detail: The spec summary lists API surfaces this concept doesn't currently name on-page. Either rename the controls / readouts to mention them, or add a second concept page that targets the missing surface directly. The reviewer extracted these from the spec: navigator.deviceMemory limits update. Removes the explicit 0.25 GiB lower and 8 GiB upper bound recommendations. UAs may now report bucketed values that better reflect modern devices (e.g. up to 32+ GiB). Device-Memory client hint follows the same buckets. Edge cases: still bucketed, not exact, to limit fingerprinting; callers must not infer exact RAM, must treat as a hint.

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one failure mode
openQuestions.detail: The page currently shows the happy path. Add a UI affordance that drives the feature into a failure mode the spec explicitly calls out (timeout, denied permission, cross-origin block, malformed input) and surface what the browser actually does.

openQuestions.title: Make this concept interactive
openQuestions.detail: Concept pages must not ship as styled code cards. Add a control surface that actually invokes the API and shows the side effect in-page.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: Every concept page must cite chromestatus.com/feature/<id> in its 'see also' or references section — this is the self-heal anchor used by the slug cleanup script.

openQuestions.title: Tighten the design-system compliance
openQuestions.detail: Either replace raw hex with --bg-*/--text-*/--accent-* tokens, or pair every button background override with a color override (the global button rule sets both — overriding one alone risks light-on-light).

summary: Concept needs follow-up work before it really lands. 5 open question(s) queued.
